intro: Orchis broteroana, also known as Brotero's orchid or Portuguese orchid, is a species of flowering plant in the genus Orchis, native to the Iberian Peninsula and northwest Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its purported health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing inflammation and protecting against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Orchis broteroana include flavonoids, such as kaempferol and quercetin, as well as phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Orchis broteroana is often used to prepare infusions, tinctures, and decoctions, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including respiratory problems and gastrointestinal disorders.
